checklist_summary.note: full checklist with per-item verdicts and evidence is in the run's Phase 2 record; the three FAILs were self-authored doc-accuracy defects, all fixed in this iteration

phase3_findings.description: docs/review-and-fix-budget.md rewrote a BEFORE comparand so the growth delta could stay unchanged, leaving the doc stating two values for one quantity
three derived cells (BEFORE always-loaded row, max-step tokens, net-reduction endpoints) left stale after the live-extension addend moved
module header and inventory claim the module uses 'only assert_eq plus devflow_run_focused_python_test' but it also calls devflow_module_allocate_owned_directory
Part-A AC3's exclusion half is not discharged: run.sh still drives lib/test python guards absent from the inventory's exclusion table, and the table states no population
coverage-map run_sh_blocks 707 named the module while 13 of 18 of that label's assertions live in run.sh, so the map misroutes a #707 change under the newly-inverted default
receiving-code-review.md defers to review-and-fix.md's section by verbatim heading with nothing pinning the pair
the retired-convention absence sweep passes when a listed file is unreadable (pin_count prints 0), so it cannot distinguish 'gone' from 'never read'
the #527/#528 blocks capture unit-test output and rm it unread, and read $? positionally
unchecked mktemp in the three sub-fixture allocations
the planted-defect fixture inherits ambient git config and its setup assertion checks rc, not the outcome
the positive control covers one of five guards while three surfaces generalize it to all of them
the absence guard is the one new guard with no mutation-taking proof that it can still count
self-referential ordinal count in a run.sh comment (the PR #553 rot class)
tier naming drifts inside review-and-fix.md's rewritten section (local/interactive + reception/shepherd vs 'a local-tier rule only')
the inventory's closing paragraph restated the coverage-map remedy the caveat section already gives
CONTRIBUTING.md untouched though the issue lists it under Documentation Needed
a stray .mutbak backup was reported in the worktree

fix_decisions.evidence: both agents that raised it also verified nothing in CONTRIBUTING.md is stale: its two run-module lines are explicitly illustrative ('you can run in isolation'), it enumerates no module count, its 6-step checklist is unaffected, and it carries none of the retired phrasings

parking_evidence.basis: the finding asks for an edit to a file the agents themselves verified is not stale; adding the module to an illustrative example list is optional polish, not a defect
parking_evidence.failing_input: a reader of CONTRIBUTING.md after this change
parking_evidence.source: CONTRIBUTING.md lines 45-56 (the examples are introduced as ones 'you can run in isolation while iterating on their area')

fix_decisions.claim_text: a stray mutation backup is left in the worktree
fix_decisions.evidence: the file does not exist: the mutation check that created it removed it in the same command, and both `ls lib/test/modules/*.mutbak` and `git status --short` show nothing — the agent observed it mid-flight while the check was running

parking_evidence.basis: transient artifact of a mutation proof that cleaned up after itself; not present in the tree or the commit
parking_evidence.source: ls + git status --short over the worktree
